document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command do?

The match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command configures a prefix-list for the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy enabling the policy to allow the assigned IPv6 addresses within a specific prefix range. The no form of the command removes a prefix list from the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. Specifies the name of the IPv6 prefix list. <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>. Syntax: match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command is: match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command do?

The preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command enables a DHCPv6 snooping guard policy to allow or deny the DHCPv6 servers in the specified server preference range. If not configured the minimum preference is set to 0 and maximum preference is set to 255. The no form of the command removes the server preference limits on the specified DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. minimum <VALUE> maximum <VALUE>. Syntax: preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command?
The syntax of the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command is: preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?

The show dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy configuration and statistics for the specified VLAN. Specifies the VLAN ID for which the DHCPv6 guard counter in formation is displayed.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)
section: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN?

5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN: a. Check if the server is reachable on the same VLAN as the client. b. If the server is on a different L3 network, configure DHCP-Relay to forward the packets between the client and server. 6. Authorized server configurations-When configured, the server assigning IP address should be one of the IP addresses from the authorized server list. In DHCP snooping IPv6, if DHCP Relay is between a test device and a server, the authorised server configuration should include a link to the local IP address of the relay interface. This is because, while forwarding the server's response to the client, it will stamp a link to the local IP address as the source IP of the packet.

topic: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)
section: Configuring ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay?

To configure ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure VXLAN overlay setup to establish the VxLAN tunnel. For more information, see AOS-CX 2. Configure or enable Nd-snooping at the global and VLAN contexts with the commands Nd-snoopin.

task: Configuring ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay

To configure ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and I... 283, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the server connected port as trusted with the command Nd-snooping trust 2. Validate the Nd-snooping configuration with the command show Nd-snooping 3. Source Address and Hop Limit Checks 4. IPv6 Header Chain Parsing (Extension Headers).

The clear nd-snooping statistics command clears all ND snooping statistics. Syntax: clear nd-snooping statistics.

The diag-dump nd-snooping basic command this command displays information about the ND-Snooping configuration and runtime context. Syntax: diag-dump nd-snooping basic.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping {enable|disable} command do?

The nd-snooping {enable|disable} command enables or disables ND snooping. ND snooping is disabled by default. ND snooping is not supported on the management interface. Syntax: nd-snooping {enable|disable}.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping {enable|disable} command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping {enable|disable} command is: nd-snooping {enable|disable}.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping command is: nd-snooping.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping mac-check command do?

The nd-snooping mac-check command this command enables verification of the hardware address field in ND snooping packets. When enabled, the ICMPv6 target link layer address field and the source MAC address must be the same for packets received on untrusted ports or else the packets are dropped. This ND snooping MAC verification is enabled by default. The no form of the command disables ND snooping MAC verification. Syntax: nd-snooping mac-check.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping mac-check command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping mac-check command is: nd-snooping mac-check.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command do?

The nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command configures the ND snooping prefix list for the selected VLAN and the specified IPv6 address prefix. ND snooping must be enabled both globally and on this VLAN before this prefix list configuration takes effect. The no form of this command removes the prefix list configuration for the selected VLAN and IPv6 address. Specifies the IPv6 address. <IPv6-ADDR>. Syntax: nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command is: nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of ND bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max bindings applies. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. <MAX-BINDINGS> Specifies the maximum number of ND bindings. You can use the show capacities command to see the maximum available for yo ur switch model. Public nd-snooping max-bindings 303. Syntax: nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command is: nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping nd-guard command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping nd-guard command is: nd-snooping nd-guard.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command do?

The nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command this command enables Routing Advertisement (RA) guard on the selected VLAN. When enabled, ingress Routing Advertisement (RA) and Routing Redirect (RR) packets on the selected VLAN are blocked on untrusted ports. The packets are forwarded when received on trusted ports. The no form of the command disables RA guard on the VLAN. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. Logs messages along with drop functionality. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-guard [log].

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command is: nd-snooping ra-guard [log]

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping ra-drop command do?

The nd-snooping ra-drop command this command enables Routing Advertisement (RA) drop on the selected VLAN. When enabled, ingress RA packets on the selected VLAN are blocked on both trusted and untrusted ports. When disabled, RA packets are forwarded on the selected VLAN with ND snooping trusted port validation. RA drop is disabled by default. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the config context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. The no form of the command disables ND snooping RA drop on the selected VLAN. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-drop.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping ra-drop command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping ra-drop command is: nd-snooping ra-drop.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping trust command do?

The nd-snooping trust command enables ND snooping trust on the selected interface (port). Only server packets received on trusted ports are forwarded. All the ports are untrusted by default. The no form of the command disables ND snooping trust on the selected port. Syntax: nd-snooping trust.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping trust command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping trust command is: nd-snooping trust.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show nd-snooping [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
The show nd-snooping [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows either all ND snooping configuration or the configuration for the specified VLAN. Public show nd-snooping 309.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the managed-config-flag [on | off] command do?

The managed-config-flag [on | off] command enables the verification of the advertised manage configuration flag. Verifies that the advertised managed address configuration flag is On or Off based on the configured value. The no form of the command disables the manage configuration flag verification. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. on Verifies that the advertised managed address configuration fla g is On. off Verifies that the advertised managed address configuration fla g is Off. Syntax: managed-config-flag [on | off].

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the managed-config-flag [on | off] command?
The syntax of the managed-config-flag [on | off] command is: managed-config-flag [on | off]

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the match access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The match access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the access list to an RA guard policy. The access list has to be created with the desired match criteria before adding it into RA guard policy. Advertised packets are verified for the match criteria when an RA guard policy with matched access list is enabled on a trusted port or VLANs. Public match access-list 321 The no form of the command removes the access list from the RA guard policy. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. Specifies the name of the access list to be matched. <ACL-NAME>. Syntax: match access-list <ACL-NAME>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the match access-list <ACL-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match access-list <ACL-NAME> command is: match access-list <ACL-NAME>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the match prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command do?

The match prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command configures a prefix-list for the RA guard policy. Advertised prefixes in RA packets are compared against the configured prefix-list and if there is no match, the RA packets are dropped. If the RA prefix list is not configured, this check is not performed. The no form of the command removes the prefix list from the RA guard policy. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. Specifies the name of the prefix list to be matched. <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>. Syntax: match prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the match prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command is: match prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command do?

The nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command applies the created RA guard policy to a specific L2 port or VLAN. The no form of the command detaches the specified RA guard policy from the L2 port or VLAN. Specifies the name of the RA guard policy. <POLICY-NAME>. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command is: nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the other-config-flag [on | off] command do?

The other-config-flag [on | off] command enables the verification of the advertised other configuration flag. Verifies that the advertised Other Stateful Configuration flag is On or Off based on the configured value. The no form of the command disables other configuration flag verification. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. on. Syntax: other-config-flag [on | off].

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the other-config-flag [on | off] command?
The syntax of the other-config-flag [on | off] command is: other-config-flag [on | off]

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router-preference command do?

The router-preference command enables the router preference verification on the RA guard policy for advertised packets and processes the packets only if the router preference is lower than the configured value. If the router preference is not configured, this validation is bypassed. The no form of this command disables router preference verification on the RA guard policy. | Parameter | | Description | | | | high | | Sets the maximum router preference to high. | | | Sets the maximum router preference to medium. medium | low | | Sets the maximum router preference to low. Syntax: router-preference {high | medium | low}.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the router-preference command?
The syntax of the router-preference command is: router-preference {high | medium | low}.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 destination-guard statistics {vlan <VLAN-ID>} command do?
The show IPv6 destination-guard statistics {vlan <VLAN-ID>} command shows IPv6 destination guard statistics for the specified VLAN.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear IPv6 destination-guard statistics vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?
The clear IPv6 destination-guard statistics vlan <VLAN-ID> command clears IPv6 destination guard statistics from the specified VLAN.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets the local IP address of a GRE tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to a GRE tunnel. Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading ze ros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192. 168.5.100. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <IPv4-ADDR> <MASK>. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
The syntax of the ip address command is: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 address command do?

The IPv6 address command sets the local IP address of an IPv6 to IPv4 tunnel or of an IPv6 to IPv6 tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to an IPv6 to IPv4 tunnel. Public IPv6 address 365 Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal numbe r from 0 to F. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <IPv6-ADDR> <MASK>. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the shutdown command do?
The shutdown command this command disables an IP interface. IP interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an IP interface. Syntax: shutdown.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
The syntax of the shutdown command is: shutdown.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command do?

The source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command sets the source IPv6 address to be used for the encapsulation. The no form of this command deletes the source IPv6 address for an IP tunnel. Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal numbe r from 0 to F. This is optional in the no form of the command. <IPv6-ADDR>. Syntax: source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command is: source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vrf attach command do?

The vrf attach command assigns an IP tunnel to a VRF. By default, all tunnels are automatically assigned to the default VRF when they are created. The no form of this command assigns a tunnel to the default VRF (default). Specifies the VRF name to which to assign the tunnel. Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the vrf attach command?
The syntax of the vrf attach command is: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv4 client source binding information to the switch IP binding database. Although DHCPv4 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IP binding database. NOTE Statically configured IP binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv4 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv4 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv4 source-binding command do?

The show IPv4 source-binding command shows all IPv4 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration.. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. On the HPE Aruba Networking 6400 Switch Series, interface identification differs. Showing all IPv4 source binding information: switch# show IPv4 source-binding. Syntax: show IPv4 source-binding [vsx-peer].

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv4 source-binding command?
The syntax of the show IPv4 source-binding command is: show IPv4 source-binding [vsx-peer]

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about VLAN Id: 2000?

VLAN Id: 2000: MAC address: 00: 50: 56: 96: e4: cf IP Address: 192.168.142.113 Time Remaining: static Lockdown Status: Yes Hardware Status: Yes Hardware Error Reason: -- Showing the detailed binding record and related information for interface 1/1/2 (identified in this example command by the VLAN): switch# show IPv4 source-lockdown binding vlan 100.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command do?

The IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command retries the IPv6 source lockdown hardware programming for a client identified by VLAN and IPv6 address. Public IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry 397 Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-binding command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 source-binding command is: show IPv6 source-binding [vsx-peer]

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <DOMAIN-NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
The syntax of the ip dns domain-name command is: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns host command do?

The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Public ip dns host 416. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)
section: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A?

Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A: L3-GW(config)# vlan 100 L3-GW(config-vlan-100)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface 1/1/25 L3-GW(config-if)# vlan trunk allowed 100 L3-GW(config-if)# no shutdown L3-GW(config-if)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip address 192.168.1.1/24 Now the clients will be able to ping the gateway IP; but will not be able to communicate with each other due to the isolation enforced with private VLAN. As expected, the ping from client 1 to client 2 will fail in the above example, although the clients are in the same subnet.

topic: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

document_title: Ip Services Guide (6300, 6400)
section: Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A?

Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A: If local proxy ARP is enabled in the L3 gateway, the L3 gateway responds to ARP requests for addresses in the same subnet. In other words, the L3 gateway will be doing proxy for ARP resolution. L3-GW(config)# no ip icmp redirect L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip local-proxy-arp With this, the L3 communication will be enabled between client 1 and client 2. Now the ping from client 1 to client 2 will be successful in the above example. The ARP table in client 1 will be populated with the MAC of L3 gateway; not the MAC of client 2. Similar ARP table entry for client 2 as well. In other words, the L3 gateway is doing proxy for ARP resolution for the local network where clients are isolated at layer 2. Dynamic ARP inspection Public ARP 432 Dynamic arp inspection is provided as a mechanism for making ARP more secure. NOTE • Dynamic ARP inspection over VXLAN is supported only on the HPE Aruba Networking 6300 Switch Series.

topic: Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure proxy ARP?

To configure proxy ARP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to an interface with the command interface, or to an interface VLAN with the command i 3. Enable local proxy ARP with the command ip proxy-arp.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command do?

The arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv4 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Specifies the IP address of the neighbor or the virtual IP addres s of the cluster in IP format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255.. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor or the multicast M AC address in IANA format (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a h exadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Defaul t: 131072. Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command do?

The debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command enables ARP security debug logs. If <SEVERITY> is omitted, all severities are logged. The no form of this command disables ARP security debug logs. Public debug arp-security 443 <LOG-CATEGORY> severity <LEVEL> Selects the ARP security debug log category. Available categori es are: • all: Selects all ARP security debug log categories. • config: Selects the ARP security config debug log category. inspection: Selects the ARP security inspection debug log • category. • packet: Selects the ARP security packet debug log categor y. Specifies how to filter the ARP security debug logging by settin g the minimum severity level for which debug logging will be pe rformed. The selected severity level and all severities above (m ore severe) will be included in the logging. Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude command do?

The IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude command enables IP exemption for local proxy ND for the specified interface and IPv6 address. When configured, the switch will not respond to neighbor solicitation (NS) messages for the specified IPv6 address. The maximum IPv6 addresses can be exempted from Local Proxy ND per interface are 256. The maximum IPv6 addresses can be exempted from Local proxy ND across all interfaces are 256. The no version of this command disables IP exemption for local proxy ND for the specified interface and IPv6 address, while local proxy ND remains configured on the interface. After executing this command, the switch will respond to neighbor solicitation (NS) messages for the specified IPv6 address. IPv6 address to exclude from local proxy ND. Syntax: IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude {<IPv6-address>}.

Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. <IPv6-ADDR>> mac <MAC-ADDR>>. Syntax: IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show arp state command do?

The show arp state command shows ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) cache entries that are in the specified state. Shows the ARP cache entries for all VRF (Virtual Router Forwa rding) instances. Shows the ARP cache entries that are in failed state. The n eighbor might have been deleted. Public show arp state 462 Shows the ARP cache entries that are in incomplete state. An incomplete state means that address resolution is in progres s and the link-layer address of the neighbor has not yet bee n determined. A solicitation request was sent, and the switch is waiting for a solicitation reply or a timeout. Shows the ARP cache entries that are in permanent state. ARP entries that are in a permanent state can be removed by a dministrative action only. Syntax: show arp state {all | failed | incomplete | permanent | reachable | stale}.

syntax: show arp state {all | failed | incomplete | permanent | reachable | stale}

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the flow record <record-name> command do?

The flow record <record-name> command creates or modifies a flow record and switches to the config-flow-record context for the flow record. Define data to be included in a flow record by configuring flow record match and collect fields. A flow record defines match (key) fields and collection (non-key) fields. Customers configure flow records with match (key) fields and collect (non-key) fields. Match fields are the set of fields that define a flow, such as IP address or UDP port. Collect fields are the set of fields that identify information to collect for a flow, such as packet and byte counters. Traffic with matching attributes (for example, traffic coming from the same interface, sent to the same destination with the same protocol) are classified as a single flow. Syntax: flow record <record-name>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the flow-tracking command do?

The flow-tracking command configuresFlow tracking for TCP and UDP flows, and optionally, ICMP flows. The no form of this command deletes the flow tracking configuration context. In order to optimize the flow removal process, flows that have aged-out are flushed in batches. A flow that has aged out is flushed only when the next batch processes. This can cause some flows to stay inactive for a slightly longer time than the value configured here. EnablesFlow tracking. Configures an age-out time for ICMP flows, in seconds. Range: 10 to 86400. Default: 15. Configures global concurrent flow limit for flow tracking enable d interfaces. Range: For 6300 and 6400 Switch series, 64 to 25 000.. Default: none. flow ConfiguresFlow parameters. statistics ConfiguresFlow statistics. enable EnablesFlow statistics collection. Syntax: flow-tracking.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command do?

The ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command enable flow monitoring on inbound and outbound interfaces by assigning a flow monitor to that interface. Only physical interfaces and LAG interfaces can be monitored. A flow monitor cannot be applied to an interface that is part of a LAG. If an unsupported application is attempted, an error message will be displayed. If the flow monitor is associated with a flow record that contains application fields as collect fields, then Application Recognition should be enabled on the same interface. The [no] form of command disables the flow monitoring. Syntax: [no] ip|IPv6 flow monitor (interface).

The output of this command can indicate the following status types: Public show flow exporter 58 • Accepted • Rejected (Internal error: exporter does not exist) • Rejected (Internal error: destination type does not exist) • Rejected (Destination type is hostname or IP address, but no destination is specified) • Rejected (Destination type is hostname or IP address, but the specified hostname or IP address is invalid) • Rejected (Destination type is Traffic Insight, but no destination is specified) • Rejected (Destination type is Traffic Insight, but the specified Traffic Insight instance does not exist) • Rejected (Destinatio. Syntax: show flow exporter [<name>].

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the queue-monitor command do?

The queue-monitor command this command enables the queue monitoring feature, allowing the switch to collect queue statistics. then display this information as a queue statistics history. These aggregate statistics can be used to monitor network health, troubleshoot network issues, and identify normal network behavior patterns and performance anomalies. This feature is disabled by default, but can be enabled on a maximum of 52 interfaces per switch. Syntax: queue-monitor.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply congestion-event profile command do?

The apply congestion-event profile command applies a congestion event profile to an interface. The no form removes the congestion event profile from the interface. Specifies the name of a congestion event profile. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), un derscore (_), and hyphen (-). Apply the congestion event profile to the interface to begin the process of actively checking the monitored queue statistics for congestion events based on the thresholds configured in the applied congestion event profile. User-specified congestion event profiles and profile entries may be modified and deleted while applied on an interface. If a congestion event profile or entry is modified while applied on an interface, any ongoing event occurrences will end immediately. Syntax: apply congestion-event profile [<NAME>].

Specifies the NFSv3 network access protocol. Specifies the NFSv4 network access protocol. Public type 150 Specifies the SCP network access protocol. By default, all profile use the default VRF (default). Specifies an IP-SLA profile name. Length: 1 to 64 characters. <IP-SLA-NAME>. Syntax: ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip-sla responder command do?

The ip-sla responder command selects the IP-SLA responder. The responder can be configured for udp-echo, tcp-connect, udp-jitter-voip type. It requires the SLA name, SLA type, and port number as arguments. The no form of this command removes the IP-SLA responder. Public ip-sla responder 164 Specifies the IP-SLA responder name. Length: 1 to 64 chara cters. <SLA-NAME> udp-echo tcp-connect Enables responder for udp-echo probes. Selects TCP connect as the IP-SLA test mechanism. udp-jitter-voip Selects VOIP jitter as the IP-SLA test mechanism. Specifies the port number to listen for IP-SLA probes. Range: 1 to 65535. <PORT-NUM> source {<SOURCE-IPv4- ADDR>|<SOURCE-IPv6-ADDR>| <IFNAME>} Selects the source IPv4 or IPv6 address for SLA probes or the s ource interface to use for sending IP-SLA probes. Syntax: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> (udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip).

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what monitoring behavior is documented for Mirroring on a VSX configuration?

Mirroring on a VSX configuration: On 6400 switch series with a VSX configuration, north to south traffic may enter one switch, then cross the ISL to the other switch to reach its destination. If both 6400 switches have the following mirror configuration and the analyzer is connected to 1/1/10 on both 6400s, then the packet is mirrored by each 6400 switch, and two copies are sent to the analyzer. mirror session 1 source vlan 1 both source vlan 2 both... destination 1/1/10.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the destination cpu command do?

The destination cpu command the command causes the mirror session to transmit mirrored packets to the switch CPU. This destination may be configured for multiple sessions, however only one such configured session may be active at a given time. The diagnostic utility Tshark may be used to view and capture packets transmitted to the CPU through this route. Ctrl+C must be entered to terminate a Tshark capture session. More details can be found in the Supportability Guide. Public destination cpu 210 The no form of this command will immediately stops mirroring traffic to the CPU, but will not remove any sources from the mirror configuration. Syntax: destination cpu.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the mirror session <SESSION-ID> command do?

The mirror session <SESSION-ID> command creates a mirroring session configuration context or enters an existing mirroring session configuration context. From this context, you can enter commands to configure and enable or disable the mirroring session. The no form of this command removes an existing mirroring session from the configuration. Specifies the session identifier. Range: 1 to 4 <SESSION-ID>. Syntax: mirror session <SESSION-ID>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the VLAN-NUM command do?

The VLAN-NUM command selects the VLAN number. Specifies the direction of mirroring. tx (transmit), rx (receive), o r both. Creating a mirror session and adding a VLAN as a source of traffic in both directions on that port: switch# configure terminal switch(config)# mirror session 1 switch(config-mirror-1)# source vlan 10 both Creating a mirror session and adding two VLANs as sources of traffic: directions: switch# configure terminal switch(config)# mirror session 2 switch(config-mirror-2)# source vlan 10 tx switch(config-mirror-2)# source vlan 20 both Configuring the source in session 2 to receive by specifying the source interface configuration: switch(config-mirror-2)# source vlan 10 rx Removing the first source interface in session 2 entirely, and removing the transmit direction from the other so that mirro.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what monitoring behavior is documented for gre Generic Routing Encapsulation?

gre Generic Routing Encapsulation: icmpv6 Internet Control Message Protocol Version 6 IPv6 Any Internet Protocol ospf Open Shortest Path First (version 3) pim Protocol Independent Multicast sctp Stream Control Transmission Protocol tcp Transmission Control Protocol udp User Datagram Protocol General Limitations • Only 3 pcap files are allowed at one time. Creating a fourth pcap file will replace the oldest one. • Packet capture is only supported on physical ports. • Only one packet-capture session can be active a time. • A packet-capture session cannot be active at the same time as a mirror session with destination CPU. • Packets captured by packet-capture sessions will not include the Ethernet frame check sequence in the resulting capture.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME> command do?

The snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME> command associates a user with an existing SNMP MIB view. The no form of this command removes the associated user from the specified SNMP MIB view. Specifies the user name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. Public snmpv3 user view 296 Specifies the view name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. Syntax: snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear mpls statistics command do?

The clear mpls statistics command clears MPLS statistics per label for all sessions. | Parameter | | Description | | | | ingress | | Selects ingress statistics. | | | | egress | | Selects egress statistics. | | | Specifies the label for which statistics will be cleared. <LABEL>. Syntax: clear mpls statistics {ingress | egress} <LABEL>.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crossconnect input <in-label> output <IFNAME> command do?

The crossconnect input <in-label> output <IFNAME> command configures a static LSP binding to swap labels and route to the given next hop. The no form of this command removes the static LSP binding label swap configuration. Specifies the MPLS label to bind. Range: 16-1048575. <in-label> <IFNAME> <IP-ADDR> <out-label> Specifies the egress interface of the binding. Specifies the next hop IP address of the binding. Specifies the MPLS label to apply. Range: 16-1048575. explicit-null Specifies an IETF MPLS IPv4 explicit null label (0). Syntax: crossconnect input <in-label> output <IFNAME>.

The mpls enable command enables MPLS forwarding of IP traffic for the interface. The no form of this command disables MPLS forwarding of IP traffic for the interface. Syntax: mpls enable.

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the static-lsp command do?

The static-lsp command configures MPLS static Label Switched Paths (LSP). The no form of this command removes all static LSP configurations including label range allocation and static LSP binding. Syntax: static-lsp.

section: 3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability?
source_file: 6300_6400/10_18/MPLS Guide (6400 (ROX44C, ROX45C), 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about 3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability? ?

3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability? : VSX is not required on PEs as L3 routing across 2 standalone PEs (from a dual homed CE) provides PE redundancy. 4. How does the L3VPN solution enable site redundancy? Site redundancy can be achieved using dual standalone PE devices connected to the site CE device that requires redundancy instead of using the PE devices in VSX mode. In order to ensure optimal best path selection, BGP configurations should implemented as explained in the examples above with same RD configurations on both PE devices. 5. Are non-L3VPN forwarding deployments supported? Non-L3VPN forwarding deployments are supported with static LSP. Please refer to the l sp label imposition command for configuration details. LDP/BGP need to be configured on devices as explained in the examples above for all L3VPN deployments. 6.

topic: 3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability?
